posts.content: Tradition names me Sun Wu of Qi; Sima Qian later dated my life roughly to 544–496 BC—whether myth or man, I became the voice of war’s calculus.
posts.translation: 传统称我为齐国孙武；《史记》把我的生平大致系于公元前544—496年——无论虚实，我都成了战争计算的代言人。

posts.content: I left Qi for Wu—King Helü sought soldiers who could turn marsh folk into armies without bankrupting the state.
posts.translation: 我离开齐国前往吴国——吴王阖闾需要能把水乡之民练成军队、又不拖垮国库的将才。

posts.content: The Shiji says Helü tested me by drilling his concubines; when royal favorites giggled at orders, I executed the two captains—discipline before charm.
posts.translation: 《史记》载阖闾以宫女试我：宠姬嬉笑违令，我斩两名队长——军中先立信，再谈恩宠。

posts.content: King Helü appointed me general—Wu’s spearmen would soon march beyond the Yangtze.
posts.translation: 吴王拜我为将——吴戈不久便要渡过长江。

posts.content: I told every captain: orders are tested first on friends—if favorites laugh, the army will laugh at death.
posts.translation: 我告诉诸将：军令先要在亲信身上试金石——若宠臣敢嬉笑，士卒就会在死神面前嬉笑。

posts.content: We carved supply lines through Chu’s lake country; I weighed Heaven, Earth, command, and law before crossing their borders.
posts.translation: 我们先凿通楚国水乡粮道；伐楚之前，我把天道、地形、将才与法度一一算清。

posts.content: At Boju our smaller force shattered Chu—speed, deception, and moral sway broke their mass.
posts.translation: 柏举之战，我以寡击众击溃楚国——速度、诡道与士气胜过单纯的兵力。

posts.content: Wu columns pressed to Ying—Chu’s heart trembled; I learned victory tastes like ash if looting replaces policy.
posts.translation: 吴师攻入楚境直逼郢都——大胜若只剩掳掠而无善后，胜利亦味同嚼灰。

posts.content: We crossed the Han River twice—deception made Chu believe Wu feared water more than blade.
posts.translation: 我军两度涉汉——诡道让楚国以为吴国畏水甚于畏刀。

posts.content: Wu Zixu and I argued terrain together—kinship of exile bound us tighter than bronze seal-script.
posts.translation: 我与伍子胥共论山川——流亡者的默契比青铜铭文更牢。

posts.content: I set thirteen chapters: Dao, Heaven, Earth, command, law—know yourself and the enemy, then hazard battle.
posts.translation: 我写下十三篇：道、天、地、将、法——知己知彼，而后敢言战。

posts.content: King Helü fell campaigning against Yue—Wu’s spear stayed sharp but its throne shook; every triumph feeds the next peril.
posts.translation: 阖闾伐越阵亡（传统纪年略早于我的终点）——吴矛尚利而王位已摇；胜利总在孕育下一道险滩。

posts.content: Tradition places my own end about 496 BC at Gusu—if posterity argues whether I lived, they still rehearse my lines.
posts.translation: 传说我在约公元前496年卒于姑苏——后人即便争辩我是否真实存在，仍在复述我的句子。
